name: Meyer's Fire
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the dry mustard in a small bowl and stir in enough Tabasco sauce to obtain a thick paste. Let stand for 3 minutes.
2. Combine the cream cheese and mayonnaise in a mixing bowl and whisk until smooth. Whisk in as much of the mustard mixture as you dare. Refrigerate, covered, until serving time. Any leftover dip will keep, refrigerated, for up to one week.
This dip was toned down with the addition of mayonnaise and cream cheese from the original recipe found in John D. MacDonald's book, Dreadful Lemon Sky. The original recipe, called Meyer's "Superior Cocktail Dip," is a potent mixture that has been known to make the unsuspecting leap four feet straight up into the air after scooping up a tiny portion on a potato chip. This dip is excellent not only for potato chips but also for all manner of fritters and crudites.
